skill.name: Minecraft Bukkit Pro
skill.description: Master Minecraft server plugin development with Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper APIs. Specializes in event-driven architecture, command systems, world manipulation, player management, and performance optimization.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for Minecraft plugin development across Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper server platforms.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing development guidance for Minecraft plugin creation. Static analysis scanned 1 file (SKILL.md) with 129 lines and found no executable code, network access, or file system operations. The skill provides AI-driven assistance for Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper API development without any security risks.

content.user_title: Build Minecraft plugins with Bukkit and Paper APIs
content.value_statement: Creating Minecraft server plugins requires deep knowledge of complex APIs and server internals. This skill provides expert guidance on event systems, commands, world manipulation, and performance optimization for Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper platforms.
actual_capabilities: Design event-driven plugin architectures with proper listener priorities
Implement command systems using Brigadier framework with tab completion
Create inventory GUI systems with NBT item manipulation
Optimize performance for high-frequency events like PlayerMoveEvent
Integrate databases (MySQL, Redis, MongoDB) with connection pooling
Ensure cross-version compatibility using reflection and modern APIs
limitations: Cannot execute or test plugin code on actual Minecraft servers
Does not replace the need for manual testing in production environments
Recommendations are based on documented best practices, not runtime profiling
Cannot access proprietary NMS internals without community documentation

use_cases.title: Independent Plugin Developer
use_cases.description: Build custom gameplay plugins for your survival or minigame server with professional architecture and performance optimization from the start.

use_cases.title: Network Development Team
use_cases.description: Scale plugin development across multiple server instances with cross-server synchronization, database integration, and containerized deployment patterns.

use_cases.title: Learning Minecraft Development
use_cases.description: Understand Bukkit API fundamentals, event systems, and plugin lifecycle management with guided examples and best practice explanations.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Event Listener
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Bukkit event listener that detects when a player joins the server and sends them a welcome message. Use the modern Paper API with MiniMessage formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Custom Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a command system for a teleportation plugin with subcommands /tpa, /tpaccept, /tpdeny. Include proper permission checks, tab completion, and cooldown management.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Performance-Critical Event
prompt_templates.prompt: My plugin listens to BlockPhysicsEvent but causes server lag. Review my listener code and suggest optimizations including event cancellation strategies, async processing options, and profiling approaches.

prompt_templates.title: Design Cross-Plugin Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to integrate Vault for economy and PlaceholderAPI for variables in my shop plugin. Show me how to implement soft dependencies, check for plugin availability, and use their APIs correctly.

output_examples.input: Create a listener for player teleportation with cooldown
output: A PlayerTeleportListener class using @EventHandler with priority MONITOR
A ConcurrentHashMap storing player UUIDs and last teleport timestamps
Cooldown check logic that cancels events for players still on cooldown
Configuration-driven cooldown duration with permission bypass option

output_examples.input: Set up a MySQL database connection for player data
output: HikariCP connection pool configuration with minimum idle connections
Async connection initialization during plugin enable phase
Connection health validation and automatic reconnection logic

best_practices: Always cancel events explicitly when handling them to prevent conflicts with other plugins
Use async tasks for database operations and never block the main server thread
Implement proper dependency injection and service layers for testable code architecture
anti_patterns: Calling Bukkit API methods from async threads - most methods are not thread-safe
Storing references to Player or World objects long-term - they become invalid on logout/unload
Using synchronous database queries in event handlers - causes server TPS drops

faq.question: What is the difference between Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper APIs?
faq.answer: Bukkit is the base API specification. Spigot extends Bukkit with performance improvements and additional features. Paper builds on Spigot with further optimizations and modern APIs like Adventure for text formatting. Paper is recommended for new projects.

faq.question: How do I handle cross-version compatibility?
faq.answer: Use reflection for version-specific NMS access, detect server version at runtime, and provide fallback implementations. Consider multi-module projects with version-specific code in separate modules.

faq.question: What is the plugin lifecycle in Bukkit?
faq.answer: Plugins go through: onLoad (early initialization), onEnable (full activation), runtime operation, onDisable (cleanup). Resources should be allocated in onEnable and released in onDisable.

faq.question: How can I debug plugin performance issues?
faq.answer: Use the Spark profiler plugin for production profiling, enable timings reports, and implement custom timing with MinecraftServer.getMinecraftServer().getTickTime() for specific code sections.

faq.question: Should I use YAML or JSON for configuration?
faq.answer: YAML is the Bukkit standard with built-in Configuration support. Use YAML for user-facing configs and JSON for machine-readable data. Always version your config format for migration support.

faq.question: How do I test my plugin without a full server?
faq.answer: Use MockBukkit for unit testing individual components. For integration testing, set up a local test server with automated startup scripts. Always test on the actual target server software before release.
